assertions.title: Response creation decodes the canonical GA text event sequence
assertions.reason: `output_items_reconcile_at_response_done` passed in `realtime_multimodal_output_contract`. The exercised transcript included `response.created`, `response.output_text.delta`, `response.output_text.done`, and `response.done`, and the test asserted `current.output_text() == "Hi"` and `terminal.output_text() == "Hi"`, confirming streamed text reconciles with the terminal payload.
observations: The only executed test in this binary passed.
Source inspection showed the named test validates the canonical output-text lifecycle and final reconciliation.

assertions.title: Error events stay structured and non-fatal
assertions.reason: The allowed command set produced no direct evidence for structured `error` event decoding or recovery on the same connection after an error. Source inspection of the executed tests found no assertions covering server `error` payload fields (`message`, `type`, `code`, `param`, `event_id`) or a post-error successful request on the same socket.
observations: `realtime_multimodal_output_contract`, `realtime_audio_contract`, and `realtime_tool_state_contract` each ran one passing test, but none exercised `error` events.
No additional commands were permitted by the assignment.

assertions.title: Known events accept additive fields and unknown event types remain lossless
assertions.reason: `audio_buffer_commit_and_truncation_preserve_conversation_state` passed in `realtime_audio_contract`. That test decodes a known `input_audio_buffer.committed` event carrying an additive `future_field` and asserts it remains a typed `InputAudioBufferCommitted` variant; it also decodes `input_audio_buffer.future_mode` and asserts it is preserved as `RealtimeServerEvent::Unknown`.
observations: The test source explicitly checks both additive-field tolerance and unknown-event preservation.

assertions.title: Audio Input Buffer, Interruption, and Truncation Flows Preserve Conversation State
assertions.reason: `audio_buffer_commit_and_truncation_preserve_conversation_state` passed in `realtime_audio_contract`. The test applies `input_audio_buffer.committed`, `speech_started`, `speech_stopped`, `conversation.item.truncated`, and `input_audio_buffer.cleared`, then asserts preserved conversation state for the committed user audio item, assistant truncation metadata, speech timing, previous item linkage, and cleared buffer state.
observations: The named test confirmed `committed_item_id == user_audio_1`, `previous_item_id == asst_1`, speech timing values, truncation metadata, and preserved transcripts/audio.

assertions.title: Tool, MCP, and Non-Text Content Events Decode Without Loss
assertions.reason: Two executed tests jointly cover this assertion. `tool_and_mcp_events_finalize_against_terminal_response` passed in `realtime_tool_state_contract`, proving tool-call and MCP argument/status events remain ordered and intact through terminal reconciliation. `output_items_reconcile_at_response_done` passed in `realtime_multimodal_output_contract`, proving non-text audio content-part events and transcript/audio accumulation are preserved through `response.done`.
observations: Tool and MCP streams accumulated arguments to final completed records.
Audio content-part, audio delta, and transcript delta events reconciled into the final response content.

assertions.title: Multimodal Output Lifecycle Reconciles Structural Events With `response.done`
assertions.reason: `output_items_reconcile_at_response_done` passed in `realtime_multimodal_output_contract`. The transcript exercised `response.output_item.added`, `response.content_part.added`, `response.output_audio.delta`, `response.output_audio.done`, `response.output_audio_transcript.delta`, `response.output_audio_transcript.done`, `response.output_text.delta`, `response.output_text.done`, and `response.done`. The test then asserted the accumulated audio bytes, transcript, and text content match the terminal response view.
observations: The test verified `audio == "YWJjZGVm"`, transcript `spoken hi`, text `Hi`, and terminal response status `completed`.

assertions.title: Tool and MCP Event Streams Finalize Against the Terminal Response State
assertions.reason: `tool_and_mcp_events_finalize_against_terminal_response` passed in `realtime_tool_state_contract`. The exercised transcript covered `response.function_call_arguments.delta`, `response.function_call_arguments.done`, `response.mcp_call_arguments.delta`, `response.mcp_call.in_progress`, `response.mcp_call_arguments.done`, `response.mcp_call.completed`, and `response.done`, then asserted the current and terminal response states agree on final arguments and completed status for both function-call and MCP items.
observations: The test confirmed function-call arguments finalized to `{"city":"Paris"}` and MCP arguments/status finalized to `{"server":"docs"}` / `completed`.

blockers.description: The restricted command set did not include any mocked cargo test that exercised structured realtime `error` events or same-connection recovery after an error.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected the executed integration test sources corresponding to the three allowed commands to confirm whether any named test covered `error` events; none did.

summary: Ran the three assigned mocked cargo validation commands successfully with isolated build artifacts (all exit code 0). Marked VAL-REALTIME-005, VAL-REALTIME-007, VAL-REALTIME-012, VAL-REALTIME-013, VAL-REALTIME-014, and VAL-REALTIME-015 as pass based on the named passing tests; marked VAL-REALTIME-006 as blocked because the allowed commands produced no direct `error`-event or post-error recovery evidence.
